source: Original plain-English summaries by the project author. References IICRC S500 by name; the standard text is not reproduced.

categories.1: Water from a sanitary source. Generally clean. Time and contact may shift to category 2 or 3.
categories.2: Significantly contaminated water that could cause discomfort or sickness if consumed. Sometimes called gray water.
categories.3: Grossly contaminated water. May contain pathogens, sewage, or harmful chemicals. Sometimes called black water.

classes.1: Least amount of water absorption and evaporation load. Slow evaporation. Limited area.
classes.2: Significant amount of water absorption and evaporation load. Wet carpet, cushions, structural components.
classes.3: Greatest amount of water absorption and evaporation load. Water from overhead, saturated walls and ceilings.
classes.4: Specialty drying situations. Materials with low porosity (hardwood, plaster, masonry) holding bound water.
